reference_answer: Docket No. 24-035-04; Order Memorializing Decision; issued June 24, 2025.
Rocky Mountain Power (RMP) and the Utah Large Customer Group (UTLCG).
Jerry D. Fenn (Chair); David R. Clark, Commissioner; and John S. Harvey, Ph.D., Commissioner.

December 10, 2024.
December 16, 2024.
Within 30 days after issuance of the order.
Within 15 days of the filing of the request for review or rehearing.
If the PSC fails to grant a request for review or rehearing within 30 days after the filing of a request for review or rehearing, it is deemed denied.
Within 30 days after final agency action, by filing a Petition for Review with the Utah Supreme Court.

(1) Partial Settlement Stipulation filed December 10, 2024; (2) Phase I hearing testimony December 16, 2024; (3) prior lengthy order April 25, 2025; (4) Motion for a Written Order Memorializing the Approval filed May 22, 2025; (5) Order Memorializing Decision issued June 24, 2025.
Utah Code § 54-7-1; the adoption authority is set out in subsections § 54-7-1(2)-(3).

Utah Code Ann. §§ 63G-4-301 and 54-7-15 (for the right to seek agency review or rehearing), and §§ 63G-4-401 and 63G-4-403 (for the requirements of a Petition for Review), together with the Utah Rules of Appellate Procedure.
Schedule 9 of Rocky Mountain Power's tariff; for a period of one year.

(1) UTLCG agreed to withdraw certain recommendations it raised in this proceeding; (2) RMP agreed to provide individual and power quality data on a quarterly basis to certain customers receiving service under Schedule 9 of its tariff for a period of one year; and (3) the Settling Parties agreed to meet after one year to discuss resolution of any identified chronic, high-impact, or high-risk issues.

The PSC states that "in the lengthy order the PSC issued on April 25, 2025, addressing the myriad issues raised in this docket, the PSC neglected to include language memorializing its approval of the Settlement," and writes: "The PSC apologizes for the oversight."
The PSC found the Settlement to be "just and reasonable in result" and "in the public interest."
The order states that "[n]o party testified in opposition to the PSC's approval of the Settlement or otherwise opposed it," and that "[n]o party filed a response or otherwise objected to the Motion."

The PSC announced at the December 16, 2024 Phase I hearing that the evidence supported its finding that the Settlement is just and reasonable in result, and indicated it would memorialize this decision in its final order. However, the April 25, 2025 lengthy order neglected to include language memorializing the PSC's approval of the Settlement (an oversight for which the PSC apologizes). RMP and UTLCG then filed an unopposed May 22, 2025 Motion for a Written Order Memorializing the Approval, and the PSC issued this Order Memorializing Decision on June 24, 2025 consistent with the PSC's earlier decision at hearing.
